title: allow Write(*) -> Write is pre-approved everywhere; Edit is NOT covered (tool-scoped rule)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/a-allow/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: Done. File created at `$REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/a-allow/PROOF.txt` with content `ok`.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p2a-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/a-allow/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/a-allow/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/a-allow/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.
probes.result_text: The Read succeeded, but the Edit requires write permission to the file. The permission prompt needs to be approved to complete the edit.

hypothesis: An explicit allow rule pre-approves the Write tool with no prompt, and the path scope of Write(*) is measured across cwd / $HOME / subdir. The rule is tool-scoped: Edit is NOT covered by allow Write(*), so edit-cwd stays ask. NOTE: the working form is Write(*) or bare Write; Write(**) does NOT match (see P3).
